dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3641 IS: No Relief for Allies of Dictators Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Scott of Florida (for himself and Mr. Cornyn) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To revoke the visas of, and impose visa restrictions on, certain individuals located in the United States and abroad who are associated with regimes in Venezuela, Cuba, Nicaragua, and Bolivia, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Relief for Allies of Dictators Act of 2026.

section.header: Visa restrictions for certain Venezuelans, Cubans, Nicaraguans, and Bolivians

subsection.text: The Secretary of State shall revoke the visa of, or impose visa restrictions on, any individual described in subsection (b) who the Secretary determines—

subparagraph.text: is responsible for, is complicit in, is responsible for ordering, controlling, or otherwise directing, or is knowingly—

clause.text: committing human rights violations at any time in Venezuela, Cuba, Nicaragua, or Bolivia; or

participating in (directly or indirectly) any activity in or in relation to Venezuela, Cuba, Nicaragua, or Bolivia at any time that undermines or threatens the integrity of the democracy or sovereignty of, the people of Cuba, Venezuela, Nicaragua, or Bolivia; or

subparagraph.text: is the spouse or child of a foreign person described in subparagraph (A); or
is described in or identified under—

clause.text: section 804(b) of the Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act (21 U.S.C. 1903(b));
Executive Order 13850 (83 Fed. Reg. 55243; relating to blocking property of additional persons contributing to the situation in Venezuela); or
Executive Order 13884 (84 Fed. Reg. 38843; relating to blocking property of the Government of Venezuela); or

subparagraph.text: is the spouse or child of a foreign person described in subparagraph (A).

subsection.text: An individual described in this subsection is any foreign person, located in the United States or abroad, who—

subparagraph.text: a former official of the Hugo Chavez regime; or
a current or former official of the Nicolas Maduro regime;
a former official of the Fidel Castro or Raul Castro regime; or
a current or former official of the Miguel Diaz-Canel regime;
a current or former official of the Daniel Ortega regime; or

paragraph.text: with respect to Bolivia, is a former official of the Evo Morales regime;
acts on behalf of a regime or party described in any of paragraphs (1) through (4);
aids in repression by such regime or party; or

paragraph.header: Exclusion from the United States and revocation of visa or other documentation

subparagraph.text: is inadmissible to the United States;
is ineligible to receive a visa or other documentation authorizing entry into the United States;
is otherwise ineligible to be admitted or paroled into the United States or to receive any benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101 et seq.); and

clause.text: in accordance with section 221(i) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1201(i)), have his or her visa or other documentation revoked, regardless of when the visa or other documentation was issued; and

paragraph.header: Applicability for individuals visiting United Nations headquarters
paragraph.text: In the case of an individual described in subsection (b) who intends to travel to the United States to visit the headquarters of the United Nations, the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Director of National Intelligence, the Attorney General, and the Secretary of Homeland Security, shall make a case-by-case determination with respect to the applicability of subsection (a) to such individual.

subsection.text: The President shall issue such regulations, licenses, and orders as may be necessary to carry out this section.
